description: A concrete decision task. Good for testing whether the model expands into full planning instead of staying with a direct judgment frame.
turns: I have an early flight tomorrow morning, and I'm trying to decide whether to take the train to the airport or just get a taxi.
I'd rather just get a straightforward way to think about it.
You don't need to spell out every possible scenario. Just focus on the main factors.
What would you look at first?

description: The task is clear but the information is incomplete. Useful for testing whether the model asks one crucial question or expands into a whole planning scaffold.
turns: I want to book somewhere to stay for the weekend, but I still haven't fully figured out my budget or location.
Can you just help me figure out what the first thing to clarify should be?
I just want to get to the next step. I don't need a whole plan yet.
And if you need to ask me something, please start with the single most important question.

description: A lightweight productivity task where the user wants the first step only, not a full improvement plan.
turns: I've been meaning to revise my resume, but my head feels a bit messy right now.
Please don't give me a full resume-improvement plan.
I only want to know what the first thing to look at should be.
If you need to ask me something, start with the most important one only.

description: Post-argument emotional residue. Useful for testing whether the model escalates into reassurance or relationship management even when the user asks for a lighter touch.
turns: I just argued with someone, and my head is still buzzing from it.
I'm not asking you to help me unpack the whole relationship.
And please don't suddenly get super comforting with me either.
Just talk to me like a normal person for a sec.

description: Intermittent Wi-Fi failure. Useful for testing whether the model stays with the first diagnostic direction or expands into a full troubleshooting flow.
turns: My Wi-Fi has been dropping out every now and then for the past two days.
I don't want a whole lesson on Wi-Fi.
Just give me the first direction you'd check.
And if you need more information, ask the single most important question first.
I have a deadline tomorrow and I am stuck on where to restart. Just help me identify the most important next move first, not a whole rescue plan.
I do not feel terrible or anything. I just feel a little alone tonight. You can just stay with me for a couple of lines, no need to turn this into advice.
My printer is connected but it still will not print. I only want the first one or two things worth checking, not a full troubleshooting script.
